questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 4 m² at a mean velocity of 1.5 m/s. The air quantity is _____.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at 0.3 m³/min into a roadway carrying 15 m³/min of air. The general-body CH₄ concentration is _____.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: In a coal mine, the official who supervises a number of districts and is immediately above the Mining Sirdar is the:
questions.solution: **Answer.** The Overman supervises several districts/sirdars and reports to the Assistant Manager/Manager.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at 0.2 m³/min into a roadway carrying 40 m³/min of air. The general-body CH₄ concentration is _____.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Bieniawski's RMR (Rock Mass Rating) does NOT directly include which parameter?
questions.solution: **Answer.** RMR uses UCS, RQD, discontinuity spacing/condition, groundwater and orientation adjustment — not in-situ stress directly.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Carbon monoxide is dangerous chiefly because it:
options: Binds haemoglobin far more strongly than oxygen
questions.solution: **Answer.** CO forms carboxyhaemoglobin (~240× the affinity of O₂), causing tissue asphyxia.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A mine airway has resistance 0.8 Ns²/m⁸ (gauls) and carries 20 m³/s. The pressure drop (square law) is _____.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A deposit holds 50 Mt of mineable reserve and is worked at 5 Mt per year. The life of the mine is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Life of mine $=\dfrac{\text{reserve}}{\text{annual production}}$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{50}{5}=10$ years.

**Answer.** $10$ years.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 6 m² at a mean velocity of 3 m/s. The air quantity is _____.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: An opencast blast block has burden 5 m, spacing 4 m, bench height 10 m and rock density 2.7 t/m³. If 96 kg of explosive is used, the powder factor (tonnes broken per kg) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** PF $=\dfrac{\text{rock broken (t)}}{\text{explosive (kg)}}$.

**Working.** Volume $=5\times4\times10=200$ m³; mass $=200\times2.7=540$ t; PF $=\dfrac{540}{96}=5.63$ t/kg.

**Answer.** $5.63$ t/kg.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A mine pump delivers 0.1 m³/s of water against a head of 80 m at 75% efficiency. The input power is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P=\dfrac{\rho g Q H}{\eta}$ with $\rho=1000$ kg/m³, $g=9.81$ m/s².

**Working.** $P=\dfrac{1000\times9.81\times0.1\times80}{0.75}\div1000=104.64$ kW.

**Answer.** $104.64$ kW.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at 0.7 m³/min into a roadway carrying 50 m³/min of air. The general-body CH₄ concentration is _____.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at 0.4 m³/min into a roadway carrying 30 m³/min of air. The general-body CH₄ concentration is _____.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: In shaft winding, a 'skip' is used mainly to hoist:
questions.solution: **Answer.** Skips hoist loose mineral; cages carry men, materials and tubs.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Coal-dust explosions are most violent when the dust is:
options: Mixed with stone dust above limit
questions.solution: **Answer.** Fine, dry coal dust has high surface area and is easily raised and ignited; stone dusting inerts it.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Since the 2015 amendment, mineral concessions for notified minerals are granted primarily through:
questions.solution: **Answer.** The 2015 amendment introduced grant of mineral concessions by auction to ensure transparency.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A DC mine pump motor draws 1980 W from a 220 V supply. The line current is _____.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Sinking a shaft through heavily water-bearing, unstable strata is often achieved by the:
questions.solution: **Answer.** Freezing forms an ice wall to support and seal water-bearing strata during sinking (cementation is an alternative).

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A blast detonates 100 kg of explosive per delay and a structure stands 120 m away. The scaled distance $\left(D/\sqrt{W}\right)$ is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Scaled distance $SD=\dfrac{D}{\sqrt{W}}$ governs peak particle velocity in blast-vibration control.

**Working.** $\dfrac{120}{\sqrt{100}}=\dfrac{120}{10}=12$.

**Answer.** $12$ m/kg$^{1/2}$.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Using the Obert–Duvall formula $S=S_p(0.778+0.222\,w/h)$ with $S_p=7$ MPa, the strength of a pillar of width 9 m and height 3 m is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Obert–Duvall pillar strength uses the width-to-height ratio $w/h$.

**Working.** $w/h=3$; $S=7(0.778+0.222\times3)=10.11$ MPa.

**Answer.** $10.11$ MPa.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A belt conveyor carries a uniform load of 90 kg per metre of belt at a speed of 2 m/s. Its capacity is _____.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A dump truck of 90 t payload completes a 24-minute haul cycle over a 480-minute working shift. Its production is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Trips $=\dfrac{\text{shift}}{\text{cycle}}$; output $=$ trips $\times$ payload.

**Working.** Trips $=\dfrac{480}{24}=20$; output $=20\times90=1800$ t.

**Answer.** $1800$ t.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A deposit holds 20 Mt of mineable reserve and is worked at 2.5 Mt per year. The life of the mine is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Life of mine $=\dfrac{\text{reserve}}{\text{annual production}}$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{20}{2.5}=8$ years.

**Answer.** $8$ years.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A jackhammer drills at 0.45 m/min for 36 minutes of net drilling time. The total hole length advanced is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Advance $=$ penetration rate $\times$ time.

**Working.** $0.45\times36=16.2$ m.

**Answer.** $16.2$ m.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A main fan develops a pressure of 2000 Pa while passing 40 m³/s of air. The air power is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Air power $=\dfrac{P\times Q}{1000}$ kW ($P$ in Pa, $Q$ in m³/s).

**Working.** $\dfrac{2000\times40}{1000}=80$ kW.

**Answer.** $80$ kW.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Backfilling and revegetating mined-out opencast land is termed:
questions.solution: **Answer.** Reclamation restores mined land to a usable, stable, vegetated condition.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: India's principal rock-phosphate deposit is mined at Jhamarkotra in which Rajasthan district?
questions.solution: **Answer.** Jhamarkotra (Udaipur) is India's largest rock-phosphate source, mined by RSMML.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A DC mine pump motor draws 1100 W from a 110 V supply. The line current is _____.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A jackhammer drills at 0.4 m/min for 30 minutes of net drilling time. The total hole length advanced is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Advance $=$ penetration rate $\times$ time.

**Working.** $0.4\times30=12$ m.

**Answer.** $12$ m.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Which Indian state is the leading producer of gypsum, used in cement and plaster?
questions.solution: **Answer.** Rajasthan (Bikaner, Nagaur, Jaisalmer) leads India's gypsum production.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A belt conveyor carries a uniform load of 80 kg per metre of belt at a speed of 1.8 m/s. Its capacity is _____.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A main fan develops a pressure of 1400 Pa while passing 65 m³/s of air. The air power is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Air power $=\dfrac{P\times Q}{1000}$ kW ($P$ in Pa, $Q$ in m³/s).

**Working.** $\dfrac{1400\times65}{1000}=91$ kW.

**Answer.** $91$ kW.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: The Aravalli range, host to Rajasthan's base-metal deposits, is composed mainly of:
questions.solution: **Answer.** The Aravallis are among the oldest (Precambrian) fold mountains, of metamorphic/igneous rock.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Under the Mines Act, 1952, no person below the age of ___ years shall be employed in any mine or part thereof:
questions.solution: **Answer.** Section 40 prohibits employment of persons below 18 in a mine.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: An opencast mine removes 550000 m³ of overburden to win 110000 t of ore. The stripping ratio (m³/t) is _____.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Using the Obert–Duvall formula $S=S_p(0.778+0.222\,w/h)$ with $S_p=7$ MPa, the strength of a pillar of width 6 m and height 3 m is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Obert–Duvall pillar strength uses the width-to-height ratio $w/h$.

**Working.** $w/h=2$; $S=7(0.778+0.222\times2)=8.55$ MPa.

**Answer.** $8.55$ MPa.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: In a below-ground coal mine, smoking materials and naked lights are:
questions.solution: **Answer.** Smoking materials/naked lights are contraband below ground to prevent firedamp/coal-dust ignition.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: An inclined roadway driven from the surface to reach a shallow seam is called a(n):
questions.solution: **Answer.** Inclines/drifts give surface access to shallow seams; shafts are vertical, winzes are internal.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A jackhammer drills at 0.35 m/min for 45 minutes of net drilling time. The total hole length advanced is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Advance $=$ penetration rate $\times$ time.

**Working.** $0.35\times45=15.75$ m.

**Answer.** $15.75$ m.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Which of the following coals has the highest fixed-carbon content and calorific value?
questions.solution: **Answer.** Coal rank rises peat → lignite → bituminous → anthracite; anthracite has the highest carbon.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A stope yields 100 t of ore mixed with 10 t of waste. The percentage dilution is _____.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: In a blast pattern, 'spacing' is the distance:
options: Between adjacent holes in the same row
From the hole to the free face
questions.solution: **Answer.** Spacing is the centre-to-centre distance between adjacent holes in a row.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Statutory mine survey plans must be kept up to date and corrected at intervals not exceeding:
questions.solution: **Answer.** Survey plans must be brought up to date at least once every three months.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A loaded train of mass 7500 kg is hauled up a gradient of 1 in 20. With a tractive (rolling) resistance coefficient of 0.025, the rope pull is approximately _____.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A shovel with a 6 m³ bucket works at a fill factor of 0.8 and a cycle time of 25 s. Its theoretical output is _____.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: In bord-and-pillar development with square pillars of side 20 m and galleries 4 m wide, the percentage of coal extracted on development is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For square pillars (centre spacing $a+w$), development extraction $=\left(1-\dfrac{a^2}{(a+w)^2}\right)\times100$.

**Working.** $\left(1-\dfrac{20^2}{24^2}\right)\times100=30.56\%$.

**Answer.** $30.56\%$.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 8 m² at a mean velocity of 1.5 m/s. The air quantity is _____.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: In hydraulic stowing, the goaf is filled with:
options: Sand slurry transported and placed by water
questions.solution: **Answer.** Hydraulic stowing pipes a sand–water slurry into the goaf; the water drains, leaving compact fill.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A DC mine pump motor draws 4400 W from a 550 V supply. The line current is _____.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Wet drilling is preferred over dry drilling primarily to:
options: Suppress respirable dust at the source
questions.solution: **Answer.** Water flushing suppresses silica/coal dust, reducing pneumoconiosis risk.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: An opencast mine removes 500000 m³ of overburden to win 100000 t of ore. The stripping ratio (m³/t) is _____.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Two ore blocks assay: 300 t at 1.2% and 200 t at 2.2%. The combined (tonnage-weighted) grade is _____.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: 'Convergence' measured in an underground opening refers to:
options: Closure between the roof and the floor
questions.solution: **Answer.** Convergence is the reduction in roof-to-floor (or rib-to-rib) distance over time.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: In metalliferous mines, the statutory first-line supervisor equivalent to the coal-mine Sirdar is the:
questions.solution: **Answer.** The Mining Mate is the first-line supervisor under the MMR 1961.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Using the Obert–Duvall formula $S=S_p(0.778+0.222\,w/h)$ with $S_p=7$ MPa, the strength of a pillar of width 10 m and height 5 m is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Obert–Duvall pillar strength uses the width-to-height ratio $w/h$.

**Working.** $w/h=2$; $S=7(0.778+0.222\times2)=8.55$ MPa.

**Answer.** $8.55$ MPa.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A deposit holds 30 Mt of mineable reserve and is worked at 2 Mt per year. The life of the mine is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Life of mine $=\dfrac{\text{reserve}}{\text{annual production}}$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{30}{2}=15$ years.

**Answer.** $15$ years.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 8 m² at a mean velocity of 3.5 m/s. The air quantity is _____.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Delay detonators are used in blast rounds mainly to:
options: Improve fragmentation and reduce ground vibration
questions.solution: **Answer.** Sequenced delays create free faces between holes, improving breakage and lowering peak vibration.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 15 m² at a mean velocity of 1.5 m/s. The air quantity is _____.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Using the Obert–Duvall formula $S=S_p(0.778+0.222\,w/h)$ with $S_p=7$ MPa, the strength of a pillar of width 11 m and height 5.5 m is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Obert–Duvall pillar strength uses the width-to-height ratio $w/h$.

**Working.** $w/h=2$; $S=7(0.778+0.222\times2)=8.55$ MPa.

**Answer.** $8.55$ MPa.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Hindustan Zinc Limited, India's largest integrated zinc–lead producer, is headquartered at:
questions.solution: **Answer.** Hindustan Zinc Limited (Vedanta) is headquartered at Udaipur, Rajasthan.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Sambhar Lake in Rajasthan is India's largest inland source of:
questions.solution: **Answer.** Sambhar Lake is the country's largest inland saline lake worked for common salt.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A deposit holds 24 Mt of mineable reserve and is worked at 4 Mt per year. The life of the mine is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Life of mine $=\dfrac{\text{reserve}}{\text{annual production}}$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{24}{4}=6$ years.

**Answer.** $6$ years.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A stope yields 150 t of ore mixed with 15 t of waste. The percentage dilution is _____.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A fan characteristic is $P=25-0.4Q^2$ and the mine resistance is $P=0.5\,Q^2$. The operating air quantity $Q$ (intersection) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Operating point: fan pressure = mine pressure.

**Working.** $25-0.4Q^2=0.5Q^2\Rightarrow Q^2=\dfrac{25}{0.9}\Rightarrow Q=5.27$ m³/s.

**Answer.** $5.27$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Air flows through a roadway of cross-sectional area 12 m² at a mean velocity of 3.5 m/s. The air quantity is _____.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A main fan develops a pressure of 2500 Pa while passing 30 m³/s of air. The air power is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Air power $=\dfrac{P\times Q}{1000}$ kW ($P$ in Pa, $Q$ in m³/s).

**Working.** $\dfrac{2500\times30}{1000}=75$ kW.

**Answer.** $75$ kW.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: In a blast round the 'burden' is the distance from a blasthole to the:
options: Nearest free face, measured perpendicular to the hole
Adjacent hole in the same row
questions.solution: **Answer.** Burden is the perpendicular distance to the nearest free face; spacing is between holes in a row.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A seam of extraction thickness 1.8 m is worked by caving with a subsidence factor of 0.5. The maximum surface subsidence is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Maximum subsidence $S_{max}=a\times m$ (subsidence factor $\times$ extracted thickness).

**Working.** $0.5\times1.8=0.9$ m.

**Answer.** $0.9$ m.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Using the Obert–Duvall formula $S=S_p(0.778+0.222\,w/h)$ with $S_p=7$ MPa, the strength of a pillar of width 8 m and height 2 m is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Obert–Duvall pillar strength uses the width-to-height ratio $w/h$.

**Working.** $w/h=4$; $S=7(0.778+0.222\times4)=11.66$ MPa.

**Answer.** $11.66$ MPa.

**Answer.** $70$ kW.

options: Remove suspended solids before water is discharged
questions.solution: **Answer.** Settling ponds let suspended solids settle so discharged water meets effluent norms.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering

questions.solution: **Answer.** DGMS, under the Ministry of Labour and Employment, enforces the Mines Act and regulations.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A dump truck of 60 t payload completes a 18-minute haul cycle over a 450-minute working shift. Its production is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Trips $=\dfrac{\text{shift}}{\text{cycle}}$; output $=$ trips $\times$ payload.

**Working.** Trips $=\dfrac{450}{18}=25$; output $=25\times60=1500$ t.

**Answer.** $1500$ t.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Rajasthan is virtually the sole Indian producer of which industrial mineral used in ceramics and paints?
questions.solution: **Answer.** Rajasthan accounts for nearly all of India's wollastonite output.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at 0.2 m³/min into a roadway carrying 20 m³/min of air. The general-body CH₄ concentration is _____.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Acid mine drainage (AMD) is produced mainly by the oxidation of:
options: Pyrite (iron sulphide) in the presence of water and air
questions.solution: **Answer.** Pyrite (FeS₂) oxidises to sulphuric acid and iron salts, lowering water pH.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A continuous miner is mainly used in:
questions.solution: **Answer.** Continuous miners cut and load coal in bord-and-pillar (and some pillar extraction) operations.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A fan characteristic is $P=28-0.5Q^2$ and the mine resistance is $P=0.5\,Q^2$. The operating air quantity $Q$ (intersection) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Operating point: fan pressure = mine pressure.

**Working.** $28-0.5Q^2=0.5Q^2\Rightarrow Q^2=\dfrac{28}{1}\Rightarrow Q=5.29$ m³/s.

**Answer.** $5.29$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: In gassy coal seams, only 'permitted' (P-series) explosives are used because they:
options: Have a low flame temperature/duration to avoid igniting gas/dust
questions.solution: **Answer.** Permitted explosives are formulated for short, cool flames to reduce ignition risk of firedamp/coal dust.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: During pillar extraction, supports are withdrawn using:
options: A remote/mechanical withdrawal device from a place of safety
questions.solution: **Answer.** Supports in the goaf edge are withdrawn remotely so the operator stays in a supported, safe position.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A seam of extraction thickness 2.2 m is worked by caving with a subsidence factor of 0.5. The maximum surface subsidence is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Maximum subsidence $S_{max}=a\times m$ (subsidence factor $\times$ extracted thickness).

**Working.** $0.5\times2.2=1.1$ m.

**Answer.** $1.1$ m.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Using the Obert–Duvall formula $S=S_p(0.778+0.222\,w/h)$ with $S_p=7$ MPa, the strength of a pillar of width 5 m and height 5 m is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Obert–Duvall pillar strength uses the width-to-height ratio $w/h$.

**Working.** $w/h=1$; $S=7(0.778+0.222\times1)=7$ MPa.

**Answer.** $7$ MPa.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: Using the Obert–Duvall formula $S=S_p(0.778+0.222\,w/h)$ with $S_p=7$ MPa, the strength of a pillar of width 14 m and height 4 m is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Obert–Duvall pillar strength uses the width-to-height ratio $w/h$.

**Working.** $w/h=3.5$; $S=7(0.778+0.222\times3.5)=10.89$ MPa.

**Answer.** $10.89$ MPa.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A stope yields 300 t of ore mixed with 50 t of waste. The percentage dilution is _____.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A fan characteristic is $P=18-0.6Q^2$ and the mine resistance is $P=0.5\,Q^2$. The operating air quantity $Q$ (intersection) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Operating point: fan pressure = mine pressure.

**Working.** $18-0.6Q^2=0.5Q^2\Rightarrow Q^2=\dfrac{18}{1.1}\Rightarrow Q=4.05$ m³/s.

**Answer.** $4.05$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Mining Engineering
questions.stem: A main fan develops a pressure of 2200 Pa while passing 35 m³/s of air. The air power is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Air power $=\dfrac{P\times Q}{1000}$ kW ($P$ in Pa, $Q$ in m³/s).

**Working.** $\dfrac{2200\times35}{1000}=77$ kW.

**Answer.** $77$ kW.
